item.description: President Donald Trump says the fragile ceasefire is still in effect even as attacks escalate between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said Iran fired missiles and launched drones at three American destroyers, calling the attacks “unprovoked.” They also said U.S. forces responded with self defense strikes targeting military facilities and drone launch sites in Iran. Iran says it was the U.S. who fired first and that its attacks were defensive. NBC’s

media:content.media:title: Trump Says US and Iran Ceasefire Stills Holds Amid New Attacks
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump says the fragile ceasefire is still in effect even as attacks escalate between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. Central Command said Iran fired missiles and launched drones at three American destroyers, calling the attacks “unprovoked.” They also said U.S. forces responded with self defense strikes targeting military facilities and drone launch sites in Iran. Iran says it was the U.S. who fired first and that its attacks were defensive. NBC’s Matt Bradley reports and Kristen Welker, moderator of Meet the Press, joins TODAY with analysis.

item.title: Trump Says US and Iran Ceasefire Stills Holds Amid New Attacks

item.description: The Justice Department is investigating a series of oil market trades that right before important moves related to the war with Iran. At least four trades worth $2.6 billion are being examined. NBC News’ Allie Canal explains the timing of the bets.

media:content.media:title: Source: DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades timed around Iran war
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department is investigating a series of oil market trades that right before important moves related to the war with Iran. At least four trades worth $2.6 billion are being examined. NBC News’ Allie Canal explains the timing of the bets.

item.title: Source: DOJ investigating suspicious oil trades timed around Iran war

item.dc:creator: Abigail Williams
item.description: The U.S. is urging the government to break with Tehran-backed militia groups following more than 600 attacks against U.S. diplomatic facilities.

media:content.media:text: An Iraqi army armored humvee vehicle is deployed to protect the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad
media:content.media:description: An Iraqi army armored vehicle is deployed near the banks of the Tigris River to protect the U.S. Embassy fortified "Green Zone" in Baghdad on March 14.

media:content.media:text: A fire burns outside the grounds of the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad
media:content.media:description: An Iraqi army armored vehicle is deployed near the banks of the Tigris River to protect the U.S. Embassy fortified "Green Zone" in Baghdad on March 14.

item.title: Iran-backed groups targeted U.S. facilities in Iraq 600 times, U.S. official says

item.dc:creator: Alexander Smith
item.description: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.

media:content.media:description: Starmer will now face increasing doubts over his future amid widespread speculation his colleagues may move against him.
Starmer will now face increasing doubts over his future amid widespread speculation his colleagues may move against him.

media:content.media:text: Wales Green Party Leader Anthony Slaughter Votes In The 2026 Senedd Election
media:content.media:description: Starmer will now face increasing doubts over his future amid widespread speculation his colleagues may move against him.

item.title: Hard-right party routs Starmer’s Labour in U.K. elections as Trump ally hails ‘historic shift’

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: The April jobs coming Friday morning is expected to show a resilient U.S. labor market despite the global energy shock from the U.S. war with Iran.

media:content.media:text: US Economy Ahead Of Gross Domestic Product Figures Release
media:content.media:description: The consensus among economists is that Friday's jobs report will show the U.S. added positions in April; but not everyone is that optimistic.

item.title: April jobs report expected to show gains despite Iran war pressure

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Facing a tough election challenge is a cost of doing business for members of Congress.

media:content.media:text: A split composite of Young Kim and Ken Calvert.
media:content.media:description: Young Kim; Ken Calvert.

item.title: Redistricting pits California Republican incumbents against each other in a fight for survival

item.description: The ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak is heading to the Canary Islands as health officials scramble to track passengers who disembarked from the ship early. It comes as new suspected cases are popping in multiple countries, including in the United Kingdom and Singapore. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdjian reports for TODAY from Tenerife.

media:content.media:title: Officials Race to Track Hantavirus After Outbreak on Cruise Ship
media:content.media:description: The ship at the center of the deadly hantavirus outbreak is heading to the Canary Islands as health officials scramble to track passengers who disembarked from the ship early. It comes as new suspected cases are popping in multiple countries, including in the United Kingdom and Singapore. NBC’s Daniele Hamamdjian reports for TODAY from Tenerife.

item.title: Officials Race to Track Hantavirus After Outbreak on Cruise Ship

item.dc:creator: Suzanne Gamboa
Mike Hixenbaugh
item.description: Daniel Savala, who preached for decades in an Assemblies of God-run college ministry, was sentenced to 30 years in prison without the possibility of parole.

media:content.media:text: Savala poses with Eli Stewart and other young followers in Alaska in 2002.
media:content.media:description: Savala poses with Eli Stewart and other young followers in Alaska in 2002.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland stands outside the courthouse before delivering his victim impact statement.
media:content.media:description: "You thought yourself invincible," Joseph Cleveland told Daniel Savala while giving an impact statement in court Thursday.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland as a young man; Daniel Savala's house in 2018.
media:content.media:description: Joseph Cleveland as a teen; Daniel Savala's house in 2018.
Ron Bloomingkemper Jr., a former Chi Alpha member who for years raised alarm about Savala's actions, attended his plea hearing.

media:content.media:text: From left, Krys Scroggins, Joseph Cleveland, Danny Clark, and far right, Ron Bloomingkemper, listen as Daniel Savala is sentenced.
media:content.media:description: Former Chi Alpha members, including Joseph Cleveland and Ron Bloomingkemper, listen as Daniel Savala is sentenced.

media:content.media:text: Joseph Cleveland is embraced after the hearing where he gave an impact statement.
media:content.media:description: Joseph Cleveland hugs a victim support specialist from the prosecutor's office after Savala's guilty plea.

item.title: Christian missionary hailed as ‘holiest man alive’ pleads guilty to abusing boys in Texas

item.description: Blue dot fever is a term coined by the internet and tabloid newspapers for when concerts are canceled due to low ticket sales. Artists like Post Malone, Meghan Trainor and the Pussycat Dolls are just some to pull the plug on live performances over prices.

media:content.media:title: Is blue dot fever the cause of tour cancellations?
media:content.media:description: Blue dot fever is a term coined by the internet and tabloid newspapers for when concerts are canceled due to low ticket sales. Artists like Post Malone, Meghan Trainor and the Pussycat Dolls are just some to pull the plug on live performances over prices.

item.title: Is blue dot fever the cause of tour cancellations?

item.description: During a visit to inspect the new renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, President Trump was asked about his focus on construction projects in Washington D.C. during an ongoing war with Iran.

media:content.media:title: Trump asked about focus on renovations during war in Iran
media:content.media:description: During a visit to inspect the new renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, President Trump was asked about his focus on construction projects in Washington D.C. during an ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: Trump asked about focus on renovations during war in Iran

item.description: President Donald Trump tells reporters that he's been briefed on the hantavirus and that it's "very much, we hope, under control."

media:content.media:title: Trump says he's been briefed on hantavirus
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump tells reporters that he's been briefed on the hantavirus and that it's "very much, we hope, under control."

item.title: Trump says he's been briefed on hantavirus

item.description: NBC News' Raf Sanchez joins "Here's the Scoop" to talk about the latest hantavirus outbreak and what the World Health Organization is saying about the possibility of the virus becoming a large-scale risk.

media:content.media:title: Could hantavirus be the next Covid? Experts weigh in.
